title: Jaylen Brown challenges Stephen A. Smith to debate at site like Harvard or MIT
author: Conor Ryan
bullets: Jaylen Brown proposes a live public debate with Stephen A. Smith at Harvard or MIT.
Brown's critique targets both Stephen A. Smith and the broader media industry he represents.
Brown and Smith's feud intensified after Boston's playoff exit against the 76ers.

title: NBA Insider: Celtics, Heat are 2 teams ‘believed to intrigue’ Giannis in a blockbuster trade
author: Conor Ryan
bullets: Giannis Antetokounmpo is linked to trade rumors involving the Celtics and the Heat, according to NBA insider Marc Stein.
A trade for Giannis would likely require the Celtics to move Jaylen Brown in a multi-team trade scenario.
Teams like the Atlanta Hawks have previously expressed interest in Jaylen Brown despite concerns about his contract size.

title: Read what Bruce Springsteen had to say about Trump, ICE, and America in Boston Sunday
author: Peter Chianca
bullets: Bruce Springsteen criticized President Trump and his administration during his Boston concert at TD Garden.
Springsteen honored victims Renee Good and Alex Pretti, who he said were killed by federal actions in Minneapolis.
He urged the audience to fight for democracy and American ideals amid current political challenges.

title: Review and setlist: Springsteen eviscerates Trump at fiery TD Garden stop
author: Peter Chianca
bullets: Bruce Springsteen criticized President Trump during his concert at Boston's TD Garden, framing it as a political rally.
The concert featured 27 songs, including a mix of Springsteen's hits and covers, highlighting themes of hope and resistance.
Guitarist Tom Morello joined Springsteen, adding intensity to performances such as "Born in the USA" and "Clampdown."

title: Review and setlist: Leslie Odom Jr. brings Broadway to the Boston Pops
author: Shannon Murphy
bullets: Leslie Odom Jr. performed with the Boston Pops at Symphony Hall on May 22, during their 140th season.
Odom Jr. sang classics like 'Fly Me To The Moon' and 'A Change is Gonna Come', showcasing his Broadway and jazz expertise.
The Boston Pops played American music staples by Rodgers, Ellington, and Gould, celebrating American musical diversity.

title: Chelsea man facing slew of assault charges for alleged violence against young children
author: Darin Zullo
bullets: Kener Bautista-Zepeda, 19, charged with multiple counts of assault on two children under age 14 in Chelsea.
Incidents include burning a child with a cigarette lighter and using a heated fork on another child.
Bautista-Zepeda allegedly assaulted a child on camera; fled to Virginia before arrest and arraignment.

title: Concord man, 73, convicted of raping 20-year-old in Sandwich restaurant bathroom in 2023
author: Darin Zullo
bullets: Elie Jammal, 73, convicted of raping a 20-year-old in a Sandwich restaurant bathroom in July 2023.
Jammal sentenced to 13.5-15 years in prison for rape, 5 years probation for assault charges.
DA noted Jammal attacked with a 'shocking sense of entitlement'; victim's bravery praised in court.

title: Newton Church, founded in 1893, is becoming condos
author: Morgan Rousseau
bullets: Historic Newton church will be converted into six condos; adjacent building to add 26 more units.
Church of the Open Word, closed in 2020, was sold for $4 million to developer Habitech Communities.
New Art Center's planned community arts hub in the church did not materialize, leading to condo conversion.

title: 5 players the Celtics could target this offseason outside of Giannis Antetokounmpo
author: Colin McCarthy
bullets: Celtics may target Kawhi Leonard, who is a potential trade candidate if Giannis Antetokounmpo is unavailable.
LeBron James, an unrestricted free agent, could be a fit for the Celtics if he leaves Los Angeles.
John Collins, Jaxson Hayes, and Coby White are alternative offseason options for boosting Celtics' roster depth.

title: 2 local college baseball programs advance to NCAA World Series
author: Kaley Brown
bullets: Bentley University advanced to the NCAA Division II World Series for the first time after defeating Molloy University.
Endicott College reached the Division III championship round for the fourth consecutive year by beating Salve Regina University.
Both Bentley and Endicott's schedules and opponents for the World Series are yet to be announced.
